storyInfo:
Thanks to the shift in public opinion done at the Duke of Caster's instigation, Enciodes is forced to take a passive stance in Kjerag.
A Victorian reporter is suddenly attacked and abducted while interviewing the people. When she awakens, she sees Ratatos, very much not dead.

attributes.content: The empty silence leaves one feeling colder than ever. The reporter's heavy bag slows her down, her footsteps upon the snow clear for all to hear.
Less people than before, even here...
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: ...Hmm?
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Miss! Sorry to bother you, can I ask you a few quick questions?
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: About?
attributes.name: Rushed Local

attributes.content: Right now, Karlan Trade—
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: I'd rather not have to hear that name.
attributes.name: Rushed Local

attributes.content: Sir Enciodes needs to come out and go over why he signed those agreements already.
attributes.name: Rushed Local

attributes.content: 'Resources for development', what rubbish. All we got for our resources was a Catastrophe. How's he going to explain that, huh?
attributes.name: Rushed Local

attributes.content: By the time the reporter finishes digesting the lady's angry words, she has already long-since left with a swish of her cloak.
...Excuse me, sir!
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Ah, you're one of those reporters from Victoria?
attributes.name: Kind Local

attributes.content: Kjerag's not been the kindest place, lately. Even us locals prefer holing up at home. You best take care.
attributes.name: Kind Local

attributes.content: It's times like these where I can't be afraid, otherwise I'm not fit to hold this camera.
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: ...But thanks for the heads-up, anyhow. What I'd like to know is, how do you see Enciodes right now, in your honest opinion?
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Hmm... Sorry, Miss, I think he's cheated Kjerag, but Victoria's worth doubting too.
attributes.name: Kind Local

attributes.content: I've seen how big the changes here have been these past few years. If you said Enciodes hasn't had any difficulty making those changes, then I'd say you're wrong.
attributes.name: Kind Local

attributes.content: If only the Great Elder of the Court could come out and say a few words, it'd all be fine—
attributes.name: Kind Local

attributes.content: A stone flies right past the tip of the reporter's nose all of a sudden, smashing the store window behind her.
Eek—!
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Get out of Kjerag, Victorian scum!
attributes.name: Angry Local

attributes.content: Enciodes, the Vine-Bead Court, us, we've all got nothing to do with you!
attributes.name: Angry Local

attributes.content: Still got the stones to take pictures?! Get the faen off our land already!
attributes.name: Angry Local

attributes.content: Nothing has left her with complicated feelings more than this moment.
She has seen the ordinary people of Kjerag slowly become more mistrustful, more on-edge.
Yet she has also seen once-trusted members of the camera crew sneak into her room to inspect her drafts and camera, over and over.
As the man picks up yet another stone, she bows goodbye to her interviewee, and runs away from the scene...
It seems her only choice is to escape... but escape to where?
Are you Victorian, miss?
attributes.name: Local Child

attributes.content: Ah... Hello there.
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Do... you hate Victoria?
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Nope, I don't.
attributes.name: Local Child

attributes.content: A dirty little hand reaches out to her, with a piece of stale Victorian bread sitting in the palm.
But my pappa told me to throw these all away. He said we wouldn't have any Victorian charity in our house.
attributes.name: Local Child

attributes.content: Then...
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: But Kjeragandr taught us to love our neighbors, and repay kindness with kindness.
attributes.name: Local Child

attributes.content: So, I wanted to give it back to you.
attributes.name: Local Child

attributes.content: As she watches the child hurry away, the reporter feels as if the dry bread in her hand is choking her throat. Her eyelids tremble, and she clenches her teeth...
Get her in the car. Don't let anyone see.
attributes.name: Indistinct Voice

attributes.content: That is the last thing she remembers hearing before she loses consciousness.
Sorry for bothering you with yet another visit, Mr. Silverash.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: Perhaps you would rather be seeing some other pretty face during all this hubbub...
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: There is no need to be so humble.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Kjerag is indebted to you for your care, and I had hoped for the chance to express my gratitude.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Hardly, hardly... It seems my long-awaited return has left me awash with churning emotion once more.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: I would like nothing more than to be the bearer of good news every time I come over, before throwing my old bones into the mountains for some peace and quiet.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: But life is a fickle thing, and it demands we improvise, does it not?
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: I do believe you are here to give me some bad news, Lord Viscount, but not in the name of Caster.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Hmph, I'd rather not get mixed up with that old lady's affairs again.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: Though based on what went on the last time I was here... It's her who doesn't want to get mixed up with me.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: With all that said, My Lord...
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Ah, yes, on to business, I know you're a busy man.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: I am here to bid you farewell, Mr. Silverash.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: If the recent shift in public opinion against Victoria has troubled you, then I can—
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: No, no, no, nothing of the sort...
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: ...I had hoped against hope that old Caster was also reaching the age where she spoke from the heart.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: That tends to bring a bit of sweetness to the tongue, no? But as you can see, things have come to this.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: Though I may seem a dotard these days, I still find myself compelled to ask you one thing...
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: Is there truly no other way?
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: You know her plans involve more than just a few mines, My Lord.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: From the moment she laid hands on Ensia, this negotiation has become wholly unacceptable to me.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: So how do you plan to handle her? Are you really prepared to cross swords with her?
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: You know better than I that if we do not keep the idea of 'crossing swords' on the table, then Kjerag may as well already be in her pocket.
attributes.name: Enciodes
Harold
attributes.content: Of course I do...

attributes.name: Harold
attributes.content: *sigh* What to say, what to say...
Knowing how the old lady is, she will never let your little tiff go now that it's reached this stage.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: I truly am worried for you, Sir Enciodes. Whatever shall you do next?
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: What is there to do but to seize the opportunity?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Since you have mentioned her, you are also an old acquaintance of Her Grace, are you not?
attributes.name: Enciodes
Harold
attributes.content: Me?

attributes.name: Harold
attributes.content: Hardly know her.
A few years back, we'd chat about family matters, and she'd always grumble to me about how none of the young Casters were worth a damn.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: The less useful they turn out to be, the more hasty she gets in doing things. Who would have guessed that she was known as a complete enigma in her youth...
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: I had a brief encounter with Her Grace a decade or so ago. Her grandeur strikes me even now.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Hah, no need to butter her up to me.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: I am fully aware that her high-and-mighty noble posturing is something she can never change.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: No matter how many schemes are brewing behind the scenes, one must always keep one's face haughty and dignified.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: Well, that's that. I'm sure your people have more urgent things to trouble you with. I should be heading off right about now.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: This talk of ours has been productive, My Lord.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: I hope I will have a more suitable opportunity in the future to invite you to Kjerag.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Haha, couldn't ask for better!
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: Ah, yes...
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: (Quietly) When you next see Her Grace, by no means tell her I've been calling her 'old' behind her back.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: ...I would never, Lord Viscount.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Atta boy.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: I too, am really looking forward to the chance to visit Kjerag again.
attributes.name: Harold

attributes.content: Yes, nobody saw us.
attributes.name: Indistinct Voice

attributes.content: It was no trouble, Matriarch. Your request is my—
attributes.name: Indistinct Voice

attributes.content: I'll bring her over right away.
attributes.name: Indistinct Voice

attributes.content: Hey, wakey-wakey!
...I...This is... I was at—
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Half a day's sleep not enough for you? Get up, our Matriarch has things she wants to ask you.
attributes.name: Browntail Guard

attributes.content: What...?
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Who... brought me here...?
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Hmph. How long will it take you to learn a gentle touch?
I-I'm sorry.
attributes.name: Browntail Guard

attributes.content: Matriarch.
attributes.name: Browntail Guard

attributes.content: Hey, you awake yet?
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: No thrashing about, unless you feel like the ropes around your wrists aren't chafing enough.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Ah, Ratatos!
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: ...Matriarch Ratatos. I thought you were—
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Not a single burn on me. Real surprising, isn't it?
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: When you get the chance, tell your colleagues that when dealing with the Browntails, best check the room for tricks.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: My... colleagues?
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Be honest now, my little spy. What were you hoping to capture on that camera of yours, all those days you were sneaking about?
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Wait, what... No, you misunderstand me, Madam! How could I be a spy?!
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: *sigh*...
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: That's what all the other spies we got said too.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: The guards rifle through the reporter's back and throw its contents onto the floor: the photos she has taken and developed the past few days.
We wouldn't care much about you if you take pictures of local traditions or even patrolling guards.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: But you didn't hesitate before taking a bunch of pictures of the Vine-Bead Court. You even went to a few mountains, clicking that shutter of yours.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Don't think I don't know anything.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Matriarch Ratatos, whether you believe me or not, I really am nothing more than a regular reporter.
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: If I was a spy, why would I be walking around with all this evidence on me?
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: With all this chaos going on right now, I could have easily given it all to my colleagues at the TV station.
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: That's what you need to explain.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Fact of the matter is, you were carrying a great deal of suspicious material, and had a very strange itinerary. How do I know you weren't trying to flee?
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Because I'm trying to protect that material!
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: The Duke of Caster wants control over public opinion. The news office sent people to join the camera crew, and they've been sneaking into my room to edit my pictures.
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: If I didn't try to get away, most of them would be confiscated, maybe even used to misrepresent things here.
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: Everything they do is to whitewash Victoria trying to strong-arm its way into Kjerag!
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter

attributes.content: If you think these pictures will be of any use to Kjerag, you can have them all.
attributes.name: Victorian Reporter
Victorian Reporter
attributes.content: I just want you to release them, to let more people know what's going on here...

attributes.name: Victorian Reporter
attributes.content: It doesn't need Victoria's 'support', but real, actual help!
That might be tricky.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Is Enciodes able to take a call right now?
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: He has more pressing matters to handle, so he sent me as support.
attributes.name: Degenbrecher

attributes.content: After my lovely funeral, I handed the spies we captured over to him. According to our agreement, he was supposed to send backup.
attributes.name: Ratatos
Ratatos
attributes.content: And in the end, he only sent one woman, even if she is the Black Knight.

attributes.name: Ratatos
attributes.content: Not sure if I should call him generous or stingy here...
So, what's his plan for dealing with Caster?
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: We're still gathering information on our enemy. I was also sent here to help with your interrogations.
attributes.name: Degenbrecher

attributes.content: The most critical thing to find out now, is if this reporter has taken any photos of 'things in the mountains'.
attributes.name: Degenbrecher

attributes.content: Ah, yes, our little friend here...
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: I can untie your restraints, but first, you need to promise me...
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: You'll answer all my questions. That sound alright to you?
attributes.name: Ratatos
